title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome Solace Brewing for our free tasting! Zach will be here to pour a selection of their new brews and warm weather crushers. Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for June.

🌟 Spyglass reaching for the stars
• Binary Stars Riwaka IPA with Citra & Riwaka
• Spatial Proximity DIPA with Mosaic, Motueka, Citra & Idaho Gem
• X-Ray Specs IPA with Strata & Rakau
• Intergalactic DDH DIPA with Galaxy, Nelson & Vic Secret
• Future Light Cone DIPA with Citra, Citra Incognito, Strata & Strata CGX

🤝 Our friends Wheatland Spring bringing the farm to Arlington
• Consequent Czech-style lager
• Mode American Lager
• Midday IPA with 100% Virginia Malt & U.S. Hops

🍃 Hops from Drowned Lands
• Silent Stars Triple IPA with 100% Citra
• Eloise West Coast DIPA with Simcoe & Citra
• Grove Juice DIPA with Nectaron, Motueka and Cryo Fresh Krush (collab with Other Half!)

🧚‍♀️ Sojourn Fermentory collabs with The Beery Godmother (ready to make the perfect half-and-half!)
• Unite Tmave Czech Dark Lager
• Together Czech Pale Lager

🔪 Slay with Hop Butcher
• The Jewels DIPA with Mosaic & Vic Secret
• Cosmic Bowl DIPA with Galaxy, Citra, Waimea & Mosaic
• Bae Pils American Pilsner

🥧 Flavor bombs from our friends at Drekker!
• We’re Gonna Need a Montage Kolsch
• Swirlee Mango Creamsicle Sour
• Slang du Jour Raspberry Cobbler Sour
• Goodbye Stranger DIPA with Citra, Galaxy, CTZ & Rakau

👊 Sunshine ready
• Schilling Schlaumeier Bavarian-style Hefeweizen
• Sojourn Paradise Pink Lady Cider

🦖 West coast vibin’ with Toppling Goliath
• Cyber Sue West Coast IPA with Citra, Mosaic, & Simcoe
• West Coast Sue IPA with Citra & Mosaic

🐳 Tiny little somethings from Maine
• Maine Beer Peeper Pale Ale with Cascade, Centennial & Magnum
• Maine Beer Little Whaleboat IPA
• Allagash Coolship Red spontaneous ferment with raspberries

🍃 East coast hops!
• Burlington Beer Co Bloom Wizard Hazy DIPA with Peaches
• Lawson’s Easy Sip IPA
• Aslin Now More Than Ever DIPA with Citra & Sabro
• The Veil Premium Sauce IPA with Galaxy, Mosaic & BRU-1

🎶 Landing from Commonwealth
• Green Room Hazy DIPA with Celeste, Citra & Azacca
• Pink Lloyd Gose with Pink Grapefruit, Lemon, Lime & Pink Himalayan Salt
• Kamehameha Imperial Stout with Banana, Coconut, Macadamia Nuts, Cacao Nibs & Vanilla Beans

⚡️Hops from Grimm
• Whoosh IPA with Simcoe, Citra & Motueka
• Light Year DDH DIPA with Galaxy, Columbus, Mosaic & Citra

🏄 New drops from Other Half!
• Lime Snaps Pilsner with Motueka &Saphir hops + Lime
• Green Diamonds DDH DIPA with Galaxy & Amarillo
• Double Mosaic Dream DDH DIPA with Mosaic, Mosaic Cryo & Mosaic Incognito

• Racquet Juice DDH IPA with Citra, El Dorado & Peacharine

🍺 Keeping pace!
• Alesmith Speedway Pace Car Nitro Coffee Stout
• Hi-Wire 10W-40 Vietnamese Iced Coffee Stout
• 21st Amendment Hell or High Watermelon Wheat
• Bell’s Hearted IPA Variety Pack featuring Change Of Heart West Coast-Style IPA, SMaSH Hearted IPA & Two Hearted IPA

🍕 New in from Old Rappahannock
• Pizza Pils Italian Pilsner
• Tight Lines Hazy IPA

🍎 Cider time with Austin East & Lost Boy
• Empire Blush Cider with McIntosh apples & a hint of strawberry
• Guava Cider
• Phantasmal Cider

Deciduous Lollipop Forest Raspberry Sour

Heavy Seas Bohdacious Blonde

Titled Barn Pooka Brown Ale

New wines in the door!

Basa Lore Igartzeta Txakoli: This Taxkoli has coastal written all over it with the proximity to the sea. Mouthwatering salinity pairs with fresh fruit and thirst quenching acidity. My favorite part? It's lightly sparkling!

Thread Feathers Howell Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon: After a brief hiatus, Thread Feathers is back! From the winemaker of our favorite red, The Dude, comes Thread Feathers from Howell Mountain in Napa. Consider this the best kept secret of Howell Mountain where the average bottle of Cab sells for well over $100. Big, bold and concentrated with dark fruit, tobacco and leather with structured tannins. 100% worth the splurge.

 

Basa Lore Igartzeta Txakoli: This Taxkoli has coastal written all over it with the proximity to the sea. Mouthwatering salinity pairs with fresh fruit and thirst quenching acidity. My favorite part? It's lightly sparkling!

Thread Feathers Howell Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon: After a brief hiatus, Thread Feathers is back! From the winemaker of our favorite red, The Dude, comes Thread Feathers from Howell Mountain in Napa. Consider this the best kept secret of Howell Mountain where the average bottle of Cab sells for well over $100. Big, bold and concentrated with dark fruit, tobacco and leather with structured tannins. 100% worth the splurge.

I spent last weekend in sunny Orlando (sorry all of you who suffered through that mis streak of rain) with my family, my in-laws and my cute-as-pie nieces. I adore my brother-in-law, Ken, who makes dream worthy chicken and dumplings and can whip up a gourmet meal from seemingly nothing. Ken is a Sommelier, so he's always pouring banging wine and nerding out with me. In Orlando, we drank strictly bubbles and chillable red including Mary Taylor Julienas Marine Descombe Beaujolais.

By now you probably have heard of Mary Taylor, but here's a refresher in case you forgot her incredible story. Mary Taylor grew up in a working-class Irish neighborhood near Boston, taking odd jobs including snowplow driver (!) to put herself through Boston College. After college, she worked New York wine auctions, became a store manager, a wine buyer, and a distributor rep — dragging the bag, as she puts it. Then she went to France, studied enology at the University of Bourgogne, befriended producers, and opened her eyes to the appellation-driven wines made by local vignerons. She earned an MBA at NYU Stern, worked in banking, and fine-tuned her plan before launching Mary Taylor wine in 2017. Mary has true, personal relationships with her partner winemakers including Marine Descombe who runs her family's 120+ year old vineyard in Beaujolais. Marine is pretty badass herself as one of only two women admitted to the Grand Council of the Compagnons du Beaujolais and becoming the first female winemaker ever to make the Forbes France Top 40 Women list.

Together, Mary and Marine have brought us a Cru Beaujolais from Juliénas. As the northernmost of the ten Beaujolais Cru villages, Juliénas is known for its steep, rugged slopes. Harvest is done entirely by hand, with partial destemming, fermentation with natural yeasts, and aging in cement vats. The soil in Juliénas is a combination of granite, volcanic and clay which gives the otherwise light-bodied wine power and structure. A wonderful blend of cherries and cranberries with forest floor vibes of soil and thyme. Earthy, textured and layered, Mary Taylor Julienas Marine Descombe Beaujolais is begging to be chilled below cellar temp for serving. So soak in the sunshine, pour yourself a glass of chilled Beaujolais and serve with a shockingly wonderful hodge-podge meal a la Ken.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Beth.

June Tasting Events coming your way!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
